rationale: Both correctly conclude this is a clean, complete mechanical rename with no blockers, matching the ground truth's trivial 'lgtm' approval — recall is tied since there's nothing substantive to miss. Y's precision and actionability are stronger because every line number it cites (754, 1017, 1157, 1248, 1286, 1329 in api_server.py; 2533, 2546, 2615 in the test file) exactly matches positions computable from the diff hunks, while X's test-file line citations (2638, 2651, 2720) don't match the diff's line numbers, weakening its grounding even though it explains this via a post-merge checkout. X adds a plausible but unverifiable upstream PR citation (vllm#46022) that Y doesn't attempt.
